dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR (Ban Ji-eum)
dialogue.line: Some say life is a journey. For me, it's a cycle. A never-ending loop of beginnings and endings. I've lived... 18 lives.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR (Ban Ji-eum)
dialogue.line: Each time, the memories stack. The joy, the sorrow, the love, the regrets. All of it, perfectly preserved, like an antique collection in my mind.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR (Ban Ji-eum)
dialogue.line: And then, there was the 18th. The life that changed everything.

dialogue.speaker: YOUNG SEO-HA
dialogue.line: Noona, are you really leaving?

dialogue.speaker: JOO-WON
dialogue.line: Just for a little while, Seo-ha. I'll be back before you know it. We'll go to the amusement park, remember?

dialogue.speaker: YOUNG SEO-HA
dialogue.line: Promise?

dialogue.speaker: JOO-WON
dialogue.line: Promise. Now, be a good boy for your father.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR (Ban Ji-eum)
dialogue.line: That day, the rain was relentless. And so was fate.

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: TIRES SCREECHING

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: CRASH

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: GLASS SHATTERING

dialogue.speaker: JOO-WON
dialogue.line: Seo-ha... I'm sorry. I broke my promise.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR (Ban Ji-eum)
dialogue.line: And just like that, Yoon Joo-won ceased to exist.

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: BABY CRYING

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR (Ban Ji-eum)
dialogue.line: The familiar darkness, then the blinding light. The overwhelming sensation of a new body, small and helpless.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR (Ban Ji-eum)
dialogue.line: A new name. Ban Ji-eum. A new family. A new life. But the old memories remained, sharp and clear.

dialogue.speaker: JI-EUM
dialogue.line: No... not again. I just... I just died.

dialogue.speaker: MOTHER
dialogue.line: Oh, my precious Ji-eum. You're finally here.

dialogue.speaker: JI-EUM
dialogue.line: Seo-ha... Seo-ha! I have to find him. I *have* to.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR (Ban Ji-eum)
dialogue.line: From the moment I could walk, I was different. My 'childish' drawings were architectural blueprints. My 'playtime' was spent devouring encyclopedias. And my 'imaginary friends' were often figures from my previous lives, offering unsolicited advice.

dialogue.speaker: YOUNG JI-EUM
dialogue.line: No, no, the optimal strategy for this stock market fluctuation is clearly a short-sell on the tech sector, not a long-term hold. Honestly, these analysts from 1998 had no foresight.

dialogue.speaker: MOTHER
dialogue.line: Ji-eum-ah, why aren't you playing with the other children? They're discussing the new cartoon.

dialogue.speaker: YOUNG JI-EUM
dialogue.line: Eomma, I'm researching corporate mergers. And besides, I already know how that cartoon ends. It's a rehash of a 17th-century French folktale, poorly adapted.

dialogue.speaker: MOTHER
dialogue.line: Oh, my unique little girl... (sighs fondly) You're going to be someone very special.

dialogue.speaker: JI-EUM
dialogue.line: Special, yes. But not for fame or fortune. Only for him.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR (Ban Ji-eum)
dialogue.line: Every decision, every study session, every skill I acquired, was a calculated step towards one person. Moon Seo-ha.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR (Ban Ji-eum)
dialogue.line: I learned to play the piano because he loved music. I learned business because his family ran a powerful conglomerate. I even learned to cook his favorite dishes, just in case.

dialogue.speaker: JI-EUM
dialogue.line: Alright, Ban Ji-eum. You're 25. You've built yourself from the ground up. You're fluent in four languages, a certified accountant, and have a black belt in taekwondo from your 14th life. You're ready.

dialogue.speaker: JI-EUM
dialogue.line: I know he's still alive. I felt it. But finding him... after all these years, after the trauma... will he even remember Joo-won? Will he be okay?

dialogue.speaker: JI-EUM
dialogue.line: It doesn't matter. I promised. And this time, I won't break it. I'll make him happy.

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: DOOR OPENS

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: FOOTSTEPS ECHOING

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR (Ban Ji-eum)
dialogue.line: My target: MI Group. His family's conglomerate. My new job application: accepted. My mission: reunite with Moon Seo-ha. And maybe, just maybe, make him smile again.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR (Ban Ji-eum)
dialogue.line: The 19th life. This time, it's not about letting go. It's about holding on. And finding my way back to him, no matter what.

summary: Ban Ji-eum, remembering all her past 18 lives, recounts the tragic end of her 18th life as Yoon Joo-won and her promise to Moon Seo-ha. Reborn as Ban Ji-eum, she dedicates her childhood and youth to becoming successful enough to find and reunite with Seo-ha.
